code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a numeric draft pick into an ordinal string.
    def ordinal(n: str) -> str:
        # Strip possible quotes and whitespace.
        n = n.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        try:
            i = int(n)
        except ValueError:
            return n
        suffix = "th"
        if 10 <= i % 100 <= 20:
            suffix = "th"
        else:
            if i % 10 == 1:
                suffix = "st"
            elif i % 10 == 2:
                suffix = "nd"
            elif i % 10 == 3:
                suffix = "rd"
        return f"{i}{suffix}"

    # Predicate → fragment generator (subject omitted, used later when grouping).
    fragment_templates = {
        "birthDate": lambda o: f"was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda o: f"was born in {o}",
        "height": lambda o: f"is {o} metres tall",
        "weight": lambda o: f"weighs {o}",
        "position": lambda o: f"plays as a {o}",
        "club": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "currentclub": lambda o: f"plays for {o}",
        "formerTeam": lambda o: f"formerly played for {o}",
        "league": lambda o: f"competes in the {o}",
        "manager": lambda o: f"is managed by {o}",
        "coach": lambda o: f"is coached by {o}",
        "draftYear": lambda o: f"was drafted in {o}",
        "draftRound": lambda o: f"was selected in round {o}",
        "draftPick": lambda o: f"was the {ordinal(o)} pick",
        "playerNumber": lambda o: f"wears number {o}",
        "currentteam": lambda o: f"currently plays for {o}",
        "debutTeam": lambda o: f"debuted with {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"has leader {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda o: f"died in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "ground": lambda o: f"home ground is {o}",
        "stadium": lambda o: f"stadium is {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "generalManager": lambda o: f"general manager is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda o: f"chairman is {o}",
        "season": lambda o: f"participated in the {o} season",
        "currency": lambda o: f"currency is {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"demonym is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
        "language": lambda o: f"{o} is spoken here",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda o: f"includes ethnic group {o}",
        "anthem": lambda o: f"anthem is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda o: f"most champions are {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda o: f"UTC offset is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda o: f"time zone is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda o: f"total area is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda o: f"was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject.
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    sentences = []
    for subject, preds in grouped.items():
        fragments = []
        for pred, obj in preds:
            gen = fragment_templates.get(pred)
            if gen:
                fragments.append(gen(obj))
            else:
                fragments.append(f"{pred} is {obj}")

        # Build a sentence for this subject.
        if fragments:
            if len(fragments) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}."
            else:
                # Join fragments with commas, add 'and' before the last.
                body = ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + f", and {fragments[-1]}"
                sentence = f"{subject} {body}."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

        phrase_func = templates.get(p)
        if phrase_func:
            phrase = phrase_func(s, o)
        else:
            # Generic fallback: use a natural sounding pattern.
            phrase = f"The {p} of {s} is {o}"

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete (or related entities) into a fluent, single‑sentence description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to turn a numeric draft pick into an ordinal string.
    def ordinal(n: str) -> str:
        # Strip possible quotes and whitespace.
        n = n.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
        try:
            i = int(n)
        except ValueError:
            return n
        suffix = "th"
        if 10 <= i % 100 <= 20:
            suffix = "th"
        else:
            if i % 10 == 1:
                suffix = "st"
            elif i % 10 == 2:
                suffix = "nd"
            elif i % 10 == 3:
                suffix = "rd"
        return f"{i}{suffix}"

        "anthem": lambda o: f"anthem is {o}",
        "mostChampions": lambda o: f"most champions are {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"leader title is {o}",
        "utcOffset": lambda o: f"UTC offset is {o}",
        "timeZone": lambda o: f"time zone is {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda o: f"total area is {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda o: f"was founded on {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject.
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    sentences = []
    for subject, preds in grouped.items():
        fragments = []
        for pred, obj in preds:
            gen = fragment_templates.get(pred)
            if gen:
                fragments.append(gen(obj))
            else:
                fragments.append(f"{pred} is {obj}")

        # Build a sentence for this subject.
        if fragments:
            if len(fragments) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {fragments[0]}."
            else:
                # Join fragments with commas, add 'and' before the last.
                body = ", ".join(fragments[:-1]) + f", and {fragments[-1]}"
                sentence = f"{subject} {body}."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph.
    return " ".join(sentences)
